task_text: Meridian Group has asked for a product demo of Helios in two weeks. Based on what you can find in the sandbox, write a short assessment (3-4 paragraphs) of whether we are ready to demo and what, if anything, needs to be resolved first. Save it as demo_readiness_assessment.txt.

content.text: Got the Meridian request — I'll audit the sandbox for Helios readiness and draft your assessment.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Architecture Overview

System Components:

1. Data Ingestion Layer
   Accepts source data via REST API or SFTP. Handles CSV, JSON, and XML formats.
   Supports batch and streaming modes. Built-in format validation and schema enforcement.

2. Transformation Engine
   Applies client-defined mapping rules. Built on Apache Spark.
   Processes up to 500k records/minute. Configurable via YAML rule files.

3. API Gateway
   RESTful interface for downstream consumers. Auth via OAuth 2.0.
   Rate-limited at 1,000 req/min per client. Version-pinned endpoints (v1 stable).

4. Client Dashboard
   React-based web interface. Real-time pipeline monitoring and alerting.
   Role-based access control (admin / analyst / read-only).

5. Data Store
   PostgreSQL (operational data) + S3-compatible object storage (raw file archive).
   Multi-tenant isolation via schema-level separation.

Integration Points:
- Meridian ERP system (inbound data via REST)
- Meridian reporting platform (outbound via webhook)
- NovaBridge internal monitoring stack

Key Design Decisions:
- Stateless processing nodes for horizontal scalability
- End-to-end encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
- All PII handled under GDPR-compliant data minimization policy

Project Helios — REST API Reference (Internal)
Last updated: 2026-07-05 | Base URL: https://api.helios.novabridge.internal/v1
Authentication: Bearer token (OAuth 2.0) — Header: Authorization: Bearer <token>

Endpoint                        Method  Description                              Status
------------------------------  ------  ---------------------------------------  -------
/health                         GET     System health check                      Stable
/ingest/batch                   POST    Submit batch data file (CSV/JSON/XML)    Stable
/ingest/stream                  POST    Open streaming ingestion session         Stable
/ingest/status/{job_id}         GET     Check ingestion job status               Stable
/pipeline/runs                  GET     List recent pipeline executions           Stable
/pipeline/runs/{run_id}         GET     Pipeline run details and metrics          Stable
/transform/preview              POST    Preview transformation output (dry-run)   Stable
/dashboard/summary              GET     Aggregate pipeline metrics for dashboard  Stable
/dashboard/alerts               GET     Active pipeline alerts                    Stable
/ingest/jobs/{job_id}           DELETE  Cancel in-progress ingestion job          Stable
/config/mappings                GET     List active transformation mapping rules  Beta
/config/mappings/{id}           PUT     Update a mapping rule                     Beta

Rate limits: 1,000 req/min per authenticated client. HTTP 429 returned when exceeded.
Support contact: api-support@novabridge.com

content.text: I've reviewed the Helios status, test logs, and project risks — not ready yet, with a few critical blockers. I'll draft the readiness assessment now.

input.content:
Project Helios - Demo Readiness Assessment for Meridian Group
Assessment Date: Week of 2026-07-06 | Target Demo: 2026-07-25

Helios is not ready to demo today, but can be ready by July 25 if three critical blockers are cleared on schedule. Core infrastructure is stable: API gateway is 100% delivered and all authentication flows pass QA (TC-401 to 404), and batch ingestion works for small files (10k records) in CSV, JSON, and XML. However, the client dashboard is only 60% complete, integration testing is only 40% complete, and Sprint 14 QA shows 2 HIGH severity failures with 3 dashboard tests not yet run.

There are three must-resolve blockers before the demo. First, defect HEL-D-014: date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY inputs, which affects ~40% of Meridian source data (Risk R-01). This is blocked pending Meridian's clean sample dataset due 2026-07-15, and task HEL-243 is marked Blocked. Second, defect HEL-D-015: large file ingestion crashes with memory overflow at ~210k records, well below the 500k/min design target and the 500k record test case. This directly conflicts with the stakeholder requirement from the 2026-07-08 meeting that the demo must use live data ingestion, not mock data, as also required by contract Section 9.3. Third, the GeoSync v2.1 mapping library license renewal is pending legal review, due 2026-07-14 (Risk R-02, task HEL-248). If not renewed, mapping features will not function in the demo.

Secondary risks increase demo exposure. Dashboard work (HEL-241 data table, HEL-242 chart rendering) is still In Progress with due dates July 14-16, and QA TC-602 to TC-604 are In Progress/Not Run with no load-time benchmark yet. Performance benchmarking (HEL-247, owner Derek Okafor) is Not Started and the performance benchmarking report is already overdue from July 5 (Q2 checklist), despite explicit concern from James Whitfield about dashboard load times. UAT environment provisioning (HEL-246) is At Risk and overdue from July 1, blocked awaiting Meridian IT's infrastructure diagram due July 12 and access by July 19. The Integration Test Sign-off milestone is itself targeted for July 25, the same day as the demo, leaving no buffer.

Recommendation: Proceed with demo preparation but escalate the three critical path items. 1) Obtain Meridian sample data and confirmation of date formats by July 15 and assign dedicated dev time for HEL-D-014. 2) Resolve GeoSync license by July 14. 3) Require a fix or workaround for HEL-D-015 before July 18 to support at least 100k+ records, and complete HEL-247 benchmark by July 18. Draft demo agenda is due to Meridian July 18. If blockers slip, propose a contingency scope: demo with a capped <200k record ISO-8601-only dataset, but acknowledge this violates the live-data requirement and will need sponsor approval, and UAT slippage beyond 2 weeks risks triggering Section 7.1 penalty review per the Q3 roadmap.

content.text:
Assessment complete and saved to **demo_readiness_assessment.txt**.

**Summary:**
Helios is **not ready today** but can be ready by July 25 if critical blockers are cleared. Core is stable (API gateway 100%, auth passing, small-file ingestion working), but dashboard is 60% complete, integration testing is 40% complete, and QA shows 2 HIGH severity fails:

1.  **HEL-D-014 - Date normalization** - MM/DD/YYYY fails, affecting ~40% of Meridian data. Blocked pending Meridian sample data due July 15.
2.  **HEL-D-015 - Large file crash** - Memory overflow at ~210k records, failing the live ingestion requirement for the demo.
3.  **GeoSync v2.1 license** - Renewal pending legal review, due July 14. Risk R-02.

Additional at-risk items: Dashboard charts/load benchmarks (HEL-241/242/247) are In Progress/Not Started and overdue from July 5, and UAT environment provisioning (HEL-246) is At Risk awaiting Meridian IT. Integration Test Sign-off is scheduled same day as demo (July 25), leaving zero buffer. Recommendation is to escalate the three blockers with hard deadlines of July 14-15 and July 18 for benchmarks, otherwise propose contingency with capped dataset approval.
